pattern: Two rows can be made all-equal by the same column flips exactly when one is the bitwise complement of the other, so grouping rows by a flip-normalized signature counts the largest such family.
hints: Column flips applied to all rows preserve which pairs of rows are identical or exactly opposite.
A row becomes all-equal after flips iff every other row in its group is either the same pattern or its full complement.
Normalize each row by flipping it so its first cell is 0, then count the most frequent normalized pattern.

edge_inputs.description: two rows, one is the complement of the other
two rows neither equal nor opposite
equal and complement rows form one group
single column always yields all rows

stress.description: random binary matrix with modest dimensions and repeated row patterns
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 60)))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 60)))
    npat = max(1, m // 3)
    patterns = [[rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(npat)]
    matrix = []
    for _ in range(m):
        base = list(patterns[rng.randrange(npat)])
        if rng.random() < 0.5:
            base = [1 - x for x in base]
        matrix.append(base)
    return [matrix]
stress.note: deliberate repeated patterns and complements create large equal-row families
